posts.text: West Bengal Electricity Regulatory Commission Approves 600 MW Wind-Solar Hybrid Power Procurement For CESC At Competitive Tariffs. SolarQuarter – 8 May 26 West Bengal Electricity Regulatory Commission Approves 600 MW Wind-Solar... The West Bengal Electricity Regulatory Commission (WBERC) approved CESC Limited's procurement of 600 MW of wind-solar hybrid power, enhancing its renewable portfolio. Conducted by PFC Consulting, the competitive bidding attracted six companies,... Estimated reading time: 2 minutes

posts.author: Mohak95
posts.text: Some of the positive points from the investor presentation & quarterly results reported today Q4FY26 result Revenue: Consolidated revenue grew 9% year-on-year to ₹18,927 Crore. Profit After Tax (PAT): Increased by 13% to ₹1,618 Crore, compared to ₹1,429 Crore in FY25. Reduced T&D loss: Achieved an all-time low Transmission & Distribution (T&D) loss of 6.11% in Kolkata, 11.4% in Rajasthan. Chandigarh (acquisition in Feb’25) and Rajasthan T&D loss reduction in future will improve profitability. SOLAR PROJECTS: A 300 MW solar project with CESC Kolkata is currently in the commissioning stage. This…
